hook: $77.45 billion. That's where the global PCB market lands in 2026 -- and India is the fastest-growing player in the game.
headline: India's PCB Market Hits $2.49B in 2026 as 45% CAGR Reshapes Global Supply Chains
body: The global printed circuit board market is projected to reach $77.45 billion in 2026, up from $74.12 billion in 2025. But the real story isn't the global number -- it's where the growth is concentrated.

India's PCB market is forecast to hit $2.49 billion this year, driven by a staggering 45% compound annual growth rate. Three forces are converging: India is now the world's second-largest smartphone manufacturer, 5G infrastructure rollouts are creating massive demand for high-frequency boards, and EV adoption is accelerating faster than anyone predicted.

The Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme has been the catalyst. Government backing has attracted both domestic and international fabricators to set up capacity in India. Meanwhile, China still dominates with $22.45 billion projected for 2026, but the share is shifting.

For context, Asia-Pacific contributes over 75% of total global PCB manufacturing output. But within that region, the growth differential tells the story: China grows at single digits while India's CAGR outpaces every major market by 3-5x.

The implication for hardware teams is clear -- Indian PCB fabs are scaling quality and capability at a pace that makes domestic sourcing viable for increasingly complex designs. HDI, flex-rigid, and multi-layer boards that once required overseas fabrication are now being produced competitively in Chennai, Bangalore, and Pune.
insight: By 2028, India could capture 5-7% of global PCB production. Companies building supplier relationships with Indian fabs now will have a structural cost and lead-time advantage.
source: Fortune Business Insights / IMARC Group / Manufacturing Today India
caption:
The numbers don't lie.

India's PCB market is growing at 45% CAGR while the global market averages 6.65%. That's not a rounding error -- it's a structural shift in where the world's circuit boards get made.

Three years ago, sourcing PCBs from India meant compromises. Today, Indian fabs are producing HDI boards, flex-rigid assemblies, and 8+ layer stacks that compete on quality with established Asian hubs.

The $2.49 billion figure for 2026 is just the beginning. With PLI incentives, growing EV demand, and 5G rollouts, India is positioned to become a top-5 PCB manufacturing nation by the end of this decade.

The question isn't whether to consider Indian PCB partners. It's whether you can afford not to.
